label: Diabetes mellitus
rationale: Common chronic condition; high retrieval utility across medication, lab, renal, cardiovascular, and inpatient paragraphs.

label: Type 2 diabetes mellitus
rationale: Common chronic condition; prioritize drug, lab, kidney, and complication associations.

label: Hypertensive disease
rationale: Common chronic condition and frequent comorbidity in clinical search.

label: Heart failure

rationale: Common condition family; useful bridge for kidney labs and chronic disease associations.

label: Sepsis
rationale: Common high-acuity syndrome; high utility for blood culture, lactate, antibiotics, and vasopressor evidence.

label: Septic shock
rationale: Common high-acuity syndrome in inpatient evidence; prioritize vasopressor and lactate associations.

label: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease
rationale: Common chronic respiratory condition; frequent steroid, bronchodilator, infection, and oxygen evidence.

label: Asthma exacerbation
rationale: Common acute respiratory presentation; frequent albuterol, steroid, oxygen, and monitoring evidence.

label: Acute ischemic stroke
rationale: Common emergency diagnosis; high utility for CTA, thrombolytic, thrombectomy, and neurologic deficit evidence.

label: Asthma
rationale: Common chronic condition and frequent target for drug/symptom associations.

label: Major depressive disorder
rationale: Common behavioral health condition; useful for medication and screening-questionnaire evidence.

label: Migraine
rationale: Common neurologic condition; useful for triptan, NSAID, symptom, and imaging associations.

label: Hyponatremia
rationale: Common electrolyte disorder; high utility for sodium, seizure, SIADH, and fluid restriction evidence.

label: Hemoglobin A1c
rationale: Common diabetes monitoring lab; high utility for diabetes and drug-response evidence.

rationale: Common kidney function lab; high utility for AKI, CKD, contrast, and nephrotoxin evidence.

rationale: Common infection test; high utility for sepsis, bacteremia, and antibiotic evidence.

label: International normalized ratio

rationale: Common diabetes and acute-care lab; high utility for insulin, hypoglycemia, and DKA evidence.

label: D-dimer

rationale: Common antihypertensive/renal drug; high utility for AKI, hyperkalemia, and medication-hold evidence.

rationale: Common respiratory drug; high utility for asthma, COPD, wheezing, and pulse oximetry associations.

rationale: Common steroid; high utility for COPD, asthma, autoimmune, and inflammation evidence.

rationale: Common drug class; high utility for inflammation, asthma/COPD, colitis, and immune-toxicity evidence.

label: Vancomycin
rationale: Common inpatient antibiotic; high utility for MRSA, bacteremia, meningitis, and C. difficile evidence.
